---
title: "Desktop App"
---

# Desktop application (Linux / Windows)

GeneWeave can be distributed as **standalone binaries** — no Python installation required on the end-user machine.

## Binaries

| Binary | Role |
|--------|------|
| `GeneWeave` / `GeneWeave.exe` | Desktop GUI (Tkinter) |
| `geneweave-cli` / `geneweave-cli.exe` | Command-line annotation |
| `geneweave-viewer` / `geneweave-viewer.exe` | Local web viewer |

## Download a release

If a GitHub Release exists (tag `v*`), download the archive for your platform:

- Linux: `GeneWeave-<version>-linux.tar.gz`
- Windows: `GeneWeave-<version>-windows.zip`

Extract it, then run the GUI binary.

### Linux

```bash
tar -xzf GeneWeave-1.1.0-linux.tar.gz
./GeneWeave
```

### Windows

Unzip the archive, then double-click `GeneWeave.exe`.

## Data and cache location

| Install mode | Results / cache location |
|--------------|--------------------------|
| Portable binary (extracted archive) | Next to the executable (`results/`, `.cache/`) |
| `.deb` system install (`/opt/geneweave`) | `~/.local/share/geneweave/` |

Internet access is required to query NCBI, Ensembl, UniProt, and the other APIs.

## Debian / Ubuntu package (`.deb`)

No `fpm` needed — `scripts/build_deb.sh` uses `dpkg-deb` (standard on Ubuntu).

```bash
bash scripts/build_linux.sh   # produce dist/ binaries first
bash scripts/build_deb.sh
```

Output: `dist/geneweave_<version>_<arch>.deb`

### Install

```bash
sudo apt install ./dist/geneweave_1.1.0_amd64.deb
# or
sudo dpkg -i ./dist/geneweave_1.1.0_amd64.deb
```

After install:

| Command | Role |
|---------|------|
| `geneweave-gui` | Desktop GUI (also listed in the Softwares menu as **GeneWeave**) |
| `geneweave` | CLI |
| `geneweave-viewer` | Local web viewer |

Uninstall:

```bash
sudo apt remove geneweave
```

## Build from source (portables)

Install build dependencies, then run the platform script from the project root.

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt -r requirements-build.txt
```

### Linux

```bash
bash scripts/build_linux.sh
```

Produces:

- `dist/GeneWeave`
- `dist/geneweave-cli`
- `dist/geneweave-viewer`
- `dist/GeneWeave-<version>-linux.tar.gz`

### Windows

```bat
scripts\build_windows.bat
```

Produces:

- `dist\GeneWeave.exe`
- `dist\geneweave-cli.exe`
- `dist\geneweave-viewer.exe`
- `dist\GeneWeave-<version>-windows.zip`

The PyInstaller recipe lives in `packaging/geneweave.spec`.

::: {.callout-note}
## Virtual environment
The build scripts automatically use `.venv/` when present. Create one with `python3 -m venv .venv` if you prefer an isolated build.
:::

## Continuous integration

The workflow `.github/workflows/build-desktop.yml`:

- Builds on **Ubuntu** and **Windows**;
- Uploads artifacts for each OS;
- On a version tag (`v1.1.0`, …), publishes a **GitHub Release** with the archives.

Trigger manually (**Actions → Build desktop binaries**) or push a tag:

```bash
git tag v1.1.0
git push origin v1.1.0
```

## CLI usage of the packaged binary

```bash
# Linux
./geneweave-cli data/GeneSymbols_2.txt --limit 3
./geneweave-cli --gene RAD51,homo_sapiens --excel --json
./geneweave-viewer --open

# Windows
geneweave-cli.exe genes.txt --dry-run
geneweave-viewer.exe --dir results --open
```

## Links in the HTML report

Database IDs (NCBI, AmiGO, UniProt, …) open in a **new browser tab**.  
This avoids loading external sites inside the viewer iframe (those sites block framing).
